title: Stock Radar: Polycab India stock hits fresh record high in May – time to buy or book profits?
summary: Polycab India hit a fresh record high in May, breaking out of a two-month consolidation phase. Experts suggest short-term traders can buy the stock for a target of Rs 9,600 in the next 1-2 weeks, with a stop loss below Rs 9,000. The stock is trading above key moving averages, indicating a bullish trend.

title: Gold Steady as US-Iran Signals Keep Rate Hike Bets Simmering
summary: Gold moved in a narrow range as conflicting signals on the progress of US-Iran ceasefire talks continued to keep traders guessing over whether central banks may need to keep interest rates higher for longer to combat inflation.

title: Investors Look Beyond TSMC as AI Boom Spreads to New Winners
summary: After several years flying high as Asia’s best Nvidia Corp. proxy, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. is increasingly vying with other artificial intelligence stocks for investor attention.

title: Zhipu, Minimax Seen Joining HK Tech Gauge, Luring More AI Bets
summary: China’s hottest AI stocks may be among candidates for inclusion in Hong Kong stock gauges, opening access to trading links that may trigger billions of dollars in inflows.

title: Ola Electric’s home inverter play has a chemistry problem
summary: The company’s decision to use expensive NMC cells instead of industry-standard LFP chemistry for its lithium-ion batteries has led to a massive revenue shortfall and slowdown in its home energy storage business.

title: Beyond tech: real estate, staffing firms invest in data centres, GCC practice to meet growing needs
summary: Non-IT companies are seizing the tech wave. Partnerships like Uber and Adani are just the tip of the iceberg. As real estate firms dive into this lucrative market, the potential for growth is immense.&nbsp;
